edges.description: An unassembled, COX16-deficient Complex IV cannot transfer electrons to oxygen, impairing oxidative ATP synthesis.
edges.source: COX16 Loss and Impaired COX2 Copper Delivery
edges.target: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Impaired oxidative ATP synthesis forces anaerobic glycolysis, producing lactic acidosis.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Chronic bioenergetic failure in cardiomyocytes drives hypertrophic remodeling.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Energy deficit in high-demand neural tissue produces encephalopathy.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Accumulated lactate produces the measured lactic acidosis.
Energy failure and metabolic decompensation impair hepatocyte function, producing liver dysfunction.

nodes.description: Biallelic loss-of-function of COX16 removes a Complex IV assembly factor that interacts with newly synthesized COX2 and its copper-center-forming metallochaperones (SCO1, SCO2, COA6) and participates in copper delivery to COX2 / CuA-site formation. Loss of COX16 blocks formation of the COX2 subassembly module and produces complete loss of the holo-complex IV, i.e. failed Complex IV biogenesis with isolated Complex IV deficiency.
nodes.id: COX16 Loss and Impaired COX2 Copper Delivery

biological_processes: mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: A novel variant in COX16 causes cytochrome c oxidase deficiency, severe fatal neonatal lactic acidosis, encephalopathy, cardiomyopathy, and liver dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: The absence of COX16 protein expression leads to a complete loss of the holo-complex IV, as detected by Western blot in patient fibroblasts

evidence.reference_title: A novel variant in COX16 causes cytochrome c oxidase deficiency, severe fatal neonatal lactic acidosis, encephalopathy, cardiomyopathy, and liver dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: Lentiviral transduction of patient fibroblasts with wild-type COX16 complementary DNA rescued complex IV biosynthesis

evidence.reference_title: A novel variant in COX16 causes cytochrome c oxidase deficiency, severe fatal neonatal lactic acidosis, encephalopathy, cardiomyopathy, and liver dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: We hypothesize that COX16 could play a role in the copper delivery route of the COX2 module as part of the complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: COX16 promotes COX2 metallation and assembly during respiratory complex IV biogenesis.
evidence.snippet: COX16, a protein required for cytochrome c oxidase assembly, interacts specifically with newly synthesized COX2 and its copper center-forming metallochaperones SCO1, SCO2, and COA6

evidence.reference_title: COX16 promotes COX2 metallation and assembly during respiratory complex IV biogenesis.
evidence.snippet: These findings implicate COX16 in CuA-site formation

evidence.reference_title: COX16 is required for assembly of cytochrome c oxidase in human cells and is involved in copper delivery to COX2.
evidence.snippet: its ablation in HEK293 cells impairs COX assembly

evidence.reference_title: COX16 is required for assembly of cytochrome c oxidase in human cells and is involved in copper delivery to COX2.
evidence.snippet: Its knockdown in C. elegans produces COX deficiency

evidence.reference_title: COX16 is required for assembly of cytochrome c oxidase in human cells and is involved in copper delivery to COX2.
evidence.snippet: copper supplementation increases COX activity and restores normal steady state levels of COX subunits in COX16 knockout cells, indicating that, even in the absence of a canonical copper binding motif, COX16 could be involved in copper delivery to COX2

nodes.description: Homozygous nonsense variant abolishing COX16 protein expression and holo-complex IV assembly. Identified in two unrelated patients and the only pathogenic COX16 variant reported to date for MC4DN22.

evidence.reference_title: A novel variant in COX16 causes cytochrome c oxidase deficiency, severe fatal neonatal lactic acidosis, encephalopathy, cardiomyopathy, and liver dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: the homozygous nonsense variant c.244C>T(p. Arg82*) in COX16

evidence.reference_title: A novel variant in COX16 causes cytochrome c oxidase deficiency, severe fatal neonatal lactic acidosis, encephalopathy, cardiomyopathy, and liver dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: the homozygous nonsense variant c.244C>T(p. Arg82*) in COX16

nodes.description: Liver dysfunction accompanying the severe neonatal metabolic crisis.

evidence.reference_title: A novel variant in COX16 causes cytochrome c oxidase deficiency, severe fatal neonatal lactic acidosis, encephalopathy, cardiomyopathy, and liver dysfunction.

nodes.description: Encephalopathy as part of the COX16-related mitochondrial presentation.

evidence.snippet: hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, encephalopathy and severe fatal lactic acidosis

nodes.description: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy reported in the index COX16 patients.

evidence.reference_title: A novel variant in COX16 causes cytochrome c oxidase deficiency, severe fatal neonatal lactic acidosis, encephalopathy, cardiomyopathy, and liver dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: two unrelated patients with the homozygous nonsense variant c.244C>T(p. Arg82*) in COX16 with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, encephalopathy and severe fatal lactic acidosis, and isolated complex IV deficiency

nodes.description: Deficient Complex IV blocks electron transfer from cytochrome c to oxygen and the coupled proton pumping, collapsing oxidative ATP synthesis. High-energy tissues — heart and brain — are preferentially injured, producing hypertrophic cardiomyopathy and encephalopathy.
nodes.id: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, cytochrome c to oxygen

nodes.description: The block in oxidative phosphorylation shifts pyruvate toward lactate, producing severe, fatal neonatal lactic acidosis with systemic metabolic decompensation and secondary liver dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: A novel variant in COX16 causes cytochrome c oxidase deficiency, severe fatal neonatal lactic acidosis, encephalopathy, cardiomyopathy, and liver dysfunction.
